title: core transport: SystemTime failure silently sets ts_floor=0, causing confusing auth error
description: core/src/transport.rs computes ts_floor via SystemTime::now().duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH).unwrap_or_default().as_secs()/60. If SystemTime::now() returns an error (broken clock), unwrap_or_default() silently returns Duration::ZERO, ts_floor=0. The PoW token is mined with ts_floor=0, sent to the relay, and rejected as stale (now_floor.abs_diff(0) > 10) with a POW_STALE error that gives the user no hint their system clock is broken. Fix: return an explicit Err (and surface it to the user as 'system clock error') instead of silently defaulting to 0.
